creator: mwlucas@blackhelicopters.org
text:
Upgraded this machine to pkgng this week. Needed some updates:

# pkg audit -q
bind99-base-9.9.2.1
libxml2-2.7.8_3
perl-5.14.2_2
sudo-1.8.5.p3
sudo-1.8.5.p3

So did pkg upgrade (twice):

# pkg upgrade
Updating repository catalogue
digests.txz                                                               100% 1040KB   1.0MB/s   1.0MB/s   00:00
packagesite.txz                                                           100% 5851KB   2.9MB/s   1.6MB/s   00:02

0 packages updated, 0 removed and 23554 added.
New version of pkg detected; it needs to be installed first.
After this upgrade it is recommended that you do a full upgrade using: 'pkg upgrade'

        Reinstalling pkg-1.1.4_1 (needed shared library changed)

0 B to be downloaded

Proceed with upgrading packages [y/N]: y
Checking integrity... done
[1/1] Reinstalling pkg-1.1.4_1... done
#

# pkg upgrade -y
Updating repository catalogue
Uprgades have been requested for the following 16 packages:

        Upgrading emacs-nox11: 24.2_8,2 -> 24.3_8,3
        Upgrading libiconv: 1.14 -> 1.14_1

        Upgrading tmux: 1.6 -> 1.8
        Upgrading virtio-kmod-9.1: 0.239473 -> 0.250249
        Upgrading gettext: 0.18.1.1 -> 0.18.3

        Upgrading python27: 2.7.3_3 -> 2.7.5_1
        Upgrading sudo: 1.8.5.p3 -> 1.8.7_1
        Upgrading bind99: 9.9.2.1 -> 9.9.3.1

The upgrade will free 5 MB

65 MB to be downloaded

And now I have:

ns10~;pkg info
bind99-9.9.3.1                 BIND DNS suite with updated DNSSEC and DNS64
...

Fix: 

No clue. I would not expect pkgng to overwrite one package with
another...
How-To-Repeat: 
install bind99-base from ports. convert to pkgng. Upgrade.
